commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And Joab said to Amasa, Are you in health, my brother. And Joab took Amasa by the beard with the right hand to kiss him. God Literally, "Hail, my brother. "(Haydock) Kiss him. "In former times it was the custom, in Greece, for supplicants to touch the chin, (Pliny, xi. ) having the left hand upon the person's knees. (Homer, Iliad A. ) The touched a woman's cheeks; (Euripides, Hecuba. ) or, among the Hebrews, her chin, Canticle of Canticles ii. The Turks and Arabs still kiss the beard, with the utmost reverence. (Thevenot xxii. ; Darvieux, chap.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
